request.question: What is the title of the work, based on Philippa Gregory's historical novel series "The Cousins' War" did Max Irons take part?

title: The Kingmaker's Daughter
The White Queen (novel)
The King's Curse
The Women of the Cousins' War
The White Princess
The Queen's Fool
The White Queen (TV series)
The Red Queen (novel)
Max Irons
The Lady of the Rivers
sentences: The Kingmaker's Daughter is a 2012 historical novel by Philippa Gregory, part of her series "The Cousins' War".
It is the story of Anne Neville, wife of Richard III of England.

The 2013 BBC One television series "The White Queen" is a 10-part adaptation of Gregory's novels "The White Queen" (2009), "The Red Queen" (2010) and "The Kingmaker's Daughter", and features Faye Marsay as Anne Neville.
The White Queen is a 2009 historical novel by Philippa Gregory, the first of her series "The Cousins' War".
It tells the story of Elizabeth Woodville, queen consort of King Edward IV of England.

The 2013 BBC One television series "The White Queen" is a 10-part adaptation of Gregory's novels "The White Queen", "The Red Queen" (2010) and "The Kingmaker's Daughter" (2012), and features Rebecca Ferguson as Elizabeth Woodville.
The King's Curse is a 2014 historical novel by Philippa Gregory, part of her series "The Cousins' War".
A direct sequel to "The White Princess", it follows the adult life of Margaret Pole, Countess of Salisbury, the daughter of George Plantagenet, 1st Duke of Clarence and Isabel Neville.
The Women of the Cousins' War: The Duchess, the Queen and the King's Mother is a 2011 book by historical novelist Philippa Gregory and historians David Baldwin and Michael Jones.
It is a non-fiction work that explores the lives of three prominent women of the historical Wars of the Roses, all of whom Gregory has featured in her "Cousins' War" series of novels.
The White Princess is a 2013 historical novel by Philippa Gregory, part of her series "The Cousins' War".
It is the story of Elizabeth of York, daughter of Edward IV of England and Elizabeth Woodville, and later wife of Henry VII and mother of Henry VIII.
The Queen's Fool by Philippa Gregory is a 2004 historical fiction novel.
Set between 1548 and 1558, it is part of Philippa Gregory's Tudor series.
The series includes "The Boleyn Inheritance".

The novel chronicles the changing fortunes of Mary I of England and her half-sister Elizabeth through the eyes of the fictional Hannah Green, a Marrano girl escaping to England from Spain where her mother was burned at the stake for being Jewish.
Hannah is discovered by Robert Dudley and John Dee and subsequently begged as a fool to Edward VI.
She witnesses and becomes caught up the intrigues of the young king's court, and later those of his sisters.
As Mary, Elizabeth, and Robert Dudley use Hannah to gather information on their rivals and further their own aims, the novel can plausibly present each side in the complex story.
"The Queen's Fool" follows Hannah from ages fourteen to nineteen, and her coming-of-age is interspersed among the historical narrative (see Bildungsroman).
The book reached # 29 on the "New York Times" Best Seller list and had sold 165,000 copies within three weeks of its release.

The White Queen is a British television drama series in ten parts, based on Philippa Gregory's historical novel series "The Cousins' War" ("The White Queen", "The Red Queen", and "The Kingmaker's Daughter").
The first episode was premiered on BBC One on 16 June 2013 in the United Kingdom.
It was first broadcast in the United States on Starz on 9 August 2013.
The Red Queen is a 2010 historical novel by Philippa Gregory, the second of her series "The Cousins' War".
It is the story of Margaret Beaufort, mother of Henry VII of England.

The 2013 BBC One television series "The White Queen" is a 10-part adaptation of Gregory's novels "The White Queen" (2009), "The Red Queen" and "The Kingmaker's Daughter" (2012), and features Amanda Hale as Margaret Beaufort.
Maximilian Paul Diarmuid "Max" Irons (born 17 October 1985) is an English-Irish actor and model.
He is known for his roles in "Red Riding Hood" (2011), "The White Queen" (2013), "The Host" (2013), and "The Riot Club" (2014).
The Lady of the Rivers is a 2011 historical novel by Philippa Gregory, part of her series "The Cousins' War".
The story is narrated by Jacquetta of Luxembourg, mother of Elizabeth Woodville, and covers the reign of the Lancastrian King Henry VI.
The novel serves as a prequel to Gregory's "The White Queen" (2009), the story of Elizabeth's reign as Queen of England.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Produce a single, concise, structured first-hop evidence report as the `summary`. The summary must do all of the following and only include content that helps determine what to fetch next:

- Enumerate concrete facts found in the provided passages. For each fact include the passage source label and either a very short quoted fragment (up to about 15 words) when a decisive short fact appears or a brief paraphrase otherwise. Keep each fact to one short line.

- Explicitly identify the bridge entity(ies) and any missing or ambiguous fact(s) that prevent answering the question from the given passages. If a name or identifier in the question corresponds to multiple candidate pages present in `passages`, list every candidate variant and mark the name as ambiguous; do not choose between them.

- Prescribe concrete, prioritized next-hop retrieval targets to resolve each missing or ambiguous element. For each target, specify which page(s) or disambiguation page to fetch and what exact section, keyword, or datum to extract (for example: specific field such as birth year, office held, list membership, dates, or the section heading to check). Prioritize targets (1, 2, 3...) when helpful.

- Only produce a final answer in `summary` if every required fact for every entity is present and unambiguous in the supplied passages; otherwise do not produce a final answer and focus on retrieval guidance.

Formatting and content constraints:
- Keep the summary concise (a short paragraph or a few short bullet-like lines).
- Produce only the single output field `summary` and nothing else.
- Preserve source-linked evidence; do not assert facts without a passage label.
- Do not invent facts or assume which ambiguous candidate is intended.
- Avoid copying long verbatim spans; use short quoted fragments when decisive and paraphrase otherwise.
- When an expected authoritative page is missing from `passages`, note that absence and include that page title as a next-hop retrieval target.

Input: fields `question` and `passages`. Output: one field `summary` meeting the requirements above.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

You are to produce exactly one compact second-hop retrieval query that targets the missing bridge or supporting evidence referenced by summary_1. Follow these operational rules when constructing the single `query` string:

- Use visible anchor tokens from `question` or `summary_1` (named works, titles, artist names, years or other identifiers only if present) and append a short retrieval cue naming the bridge sought (e.g., artist, album, recording, genre, season count, passage, supporting quote).
- Emit one concise line (one string assigned to `query`), roughly ≤ 10–12 words, formatted either as a short interrogative that names the anchors or as a compact keyword string (anchors + bridge keywords).
- If summary_1 explicitly requests a missing attribute or passage, emit the focused retrieval query rather than asking for clarification.
- Do not include answers, conclusions, enumerations, provenance notes, speculative language, or user-facing explanations; do not invent entities or add tokens that are not present in the inputs.
- Include year or other identifier tokens only if they appear in the provided inputs and help disambiguate.
- Keep wording retrieval-oriented and terse. Preserve the fallback that if retrieval using this query later fails, downstream steps may request clarification.

Output contract: produce exactly one field `query` whose value is the single compact retrieval query line described above.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Instructions for producing `summary` (single field only):
1. Merge the first-hop `context` and the second-hop `passages` into one compact integrated `summary` that preserves the primary answer name(s) and only includes passage-supported facts needed to justify or disambiguate that answer.
2. If `context` already contains a concise answer (a short sentence or a name), keep that content as the first sentence of `summary` when it is consistent with facts in `passages`. If `context` lacks a concise answer, create a concise first sentence that names the primary answer(s), using the canonical labels from `context` when available and otherwise from `passages`.
3. Expand the summary only when `passages` provide explicit, matching second-hop evidence about one or more named entities referenced in `context`. When expansion is appropriate, append at most one short supporting sentence per named entity. Each supporting sentence must begin with the entity's full name as shown in the inputs and paraphrase up to a few core attributes that are directly present in the `passages` (examples: nationality, a notable award/status, a current affiliation/team, a birth date, membership/partnership relation, or an explicit founding/formation fact).
4. Keep `summary` concise: typically 1–2 sentences for a single entity; allow up to 3 sentences only when multiple distinct named entities each require one supporting sentence. Prioritize the facts most directly necessary to answer the `question`.
5. Only assert facts that are directly supported by the provided `passages` or `context`. Do not introduce new entities, dates, awards, affiliations, roles, or causal claims that are not present in the inputs.
6. Prefer paraphrase over long verbatim copying. Use short quoted phrases only when the exact short phrase (roughly ≤10 words) appears in the `passages` and quoting preserves clarity.
7. If `context` and `passages` conflict, prefer facts that are explicitly present in the `passages`: update the concise answer to match passage-supported content rather than preserving a contradicted `context`.
8. If the retrieved `passages` do not supply explicit supporting evidence for named entities in `context`, keep the `summary` concise and do not expand or speculate.
9. Output exactly one field named `summary` whose value is the integrated post-retrieval summary text. Do not output additional fields, metadata, or commentary.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the fields `answer`.

Follow these operational rules when producing `answer`:
- Use only the content of `question`, `summary_1`, and `summary_2` as your evidential basis. Do not introduce new facts or external information.
- If both `summary_1` and `summary_2` clearly and unambiguously support the same binary (yes/no) conclusion to the question, set `answer` to exactly one lowercase token: yes or no. The `answer` field must contain nothing else (no punctuation, capitalization, quotes, explanation, or additional text).
- Decide which token to emit by aggregating the visible summaries only. Treat `summary_2` as the later integrated evidence; do not let unresolved language in `summary_1` alone prevent emitting the single-token result if both summaries now unambiguously agree.
- If the summaries conflict, are ambiguous, incomplete, hedged such that the relation is unresolved, or if the question is non-binary, do not apply the single-token rule; instead follow the agent's existing final-answer fallback behavior for unresolved or non-binary cases (which may produce a fuller response or an uncertainty signal).

Produce only the `answer` field as specified.

- Next-hop retrieval (prioritized):
  1) Fetch the Max Irons page (actor) — retrieve the filmography/roles section; extract any entry for "The White Queen" or other works based on Philippa Gregory (title, year, character name).
  2) Fetch "The White Queen (TV series)" page — check the "Cast" section for Max Irons and extract his credited role and episode/year.
  3) If (2) is negative, fetch pages for other adaptations or productions of Gregory's books (e.g., "The White Princess (TV series)" cast, "The Red Queen" adaptation pages) — check cast lists for Max Irons.
  4) If needed, fetch authoritative sources (IMDb or the actor's official site/interviews) for confirmation of which Gregory-based production Max Irons participated in (exact title and year).

No final answer: Max Irons' participation is not confirmed in the supplied passages; fetch the prioritized targets above to resolve which specific work he took part in.
response.hop2_query: Max Irons role in The White Queen TV series
hop2_docs: Max Irons | Maximilian Paul Diarmuid "Max" Irons (born 17 October 1985) is an English-Irish actor and model.  He is known for his roles in "Red Riding Hood" (2011), "The White Queen" (2013), "The Host" (2013), and "The Riot Club" (2014).

The White Queen (TV series) | The White Queen is a British television drama series in ten parts, based on Philippa Gregory's historical novel series "The Cousins' War" ("The White Queen", "The Red Queen", and "The Kingmaker's Daughter").  The first episode was premiered on BBC One on 16 June 2013 in the United Kingdom.  It was first broadcast in the United States on Starz on 9 August 2013.

The Max Headroom Show | The Max Headroom Show was a television series that debuted in the UK in 1985.  It was produced by Carlton TV and aired on Channel 4, with an initial series of 13 shows.  It featured actor Matt Frewer playing the role of pseudo-computer-generated talk-show host Max Headroom.  It returned in 1986 for a second series of six episodes plus a Christmas Special.  The final series aired in 1987.

Beauty & the Beast (2012 TV series) | Beauty & the Beast is an American television series, very loosely inspired by the 1987 CBS series of the same name, created by Sherri Cooper-Landsman and Jennifer Levin that premiered October 11, 2012, on The CW.  Kristin Kreuk and Jay Ryan star in the title roles alongside Austin Basis, Nina Lisandrello, Nicole Gale Anderson, Sendhil Ramamurthy, Max Brown, Brian J. White, Amber Skye Noyes, and Michael Roark.

Michael Marcus (actor) | Michael Marcus Morgan, better known as Michael Marcus, is an English actor.  He is best known for his portrayal of Henry Tudor in the TV series "The White Queen" (2013).  He also had supporting roles in "The Physician" (2013) and "The Theory of Everything" (2014).
Night Queen | Night Queen (Gecenin Kraliçesi) is a Turkish TV show released on Star TV in 2016.  The leading roles in this series are played by Meryem Uzerli , Burak Deniz and Murat Yıldırım.

Jessica Stroup | Jessica Leigh Stroup (born October 23, 1986) is an American actress, best known for her role as Erin Silver on "90210", Max Hardy on "The Following", and Joy Meachum in "Iron Fist".  She is regarded as a scream queen for starring in the horror films "Prom Night", "Vampire Bats", "Left in Darkness", and "The Hills Have Eyes 2".
response.summary_2: The White Queen — the 2013 British television drama based on Philippa Gregory's historical novel series "The Cousins' War." Maximilian Paul Diarmuid "Max" Irons is an English–Irish actor who is known for his role in the 2013 TV series The White Queen.
